mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment readings.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive return frequency above 58% over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not predict negative 5-day returns with a statistical significance level better than 0.10 in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent annually forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 2.5 percent correlate with positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a rank IC below 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion that lifts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a frequency exceeding 52 percent across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 95.0 index threshold indicates severe manufacturing contraction and immediate earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances when INDPRO drops below 95.0.
